mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 to promote mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Listed as CLL/AML combination-use drug; rationale noted as apoptosis priming in AML

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and other kinases to disrupt FLT3-driven signaling
additional_notes: Notes MOLM-13 is FLT3-ITD driven; described as FLT3+ AML drug

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 (and AXL) signaling to suppress proliferation in FLT3-driven AML
additional_notes: Described as potent FLT3 inhibition; indicated for FLT3+ AML

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3 and RAF pathway components
additional_notes: Rationale mentions FLT3/RAF pathway effects in FLT3-ITD AML

mechanism: Broad kinase inhibition including FLT3 (and BCR-ABL) to block oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Stated as explored in AML; broad kinase inhibition incl. FLT3

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family kinases and ABL to interfere with survival signaling
additional_notes: Rationale: SRC-family signaling implicated in AML survival

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 to suppress JAK/STAT pathway signaling
additional_notes: Existing use mentioned: myelofibrosis; noted for subset activity and combination rationale

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the RAS/MAPK pathway to reduce proliferation
additional_notes: Rationale references RAS/MAPK axis suppression in AML contexts

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 to dampen PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling and growth
additional_notes: Rationale: PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway in AML

mechanism: Selectively inhibits PI3K delta to reduce downstream survival signaling
additional_notes: Rationale: PI3K signaling in hematologic malignancies

mechanism: Inhibits the proteasome leading to proteostasis stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Note: synergy reported with chemotherapy in AML

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases to alter chromatin/epigenetic programs
additional_notes: Rationale: epigenetic dysregulation in AML

mechanism: Inhibits HDACs to induce differentiation and apoptosis programs
additional_notes: Alternative name given in text: SAHA

category: HDAC modulator (weak HDAC inhibitor at high concentration)
mechanism: At high concentrations has HDAC-inhibitory activity leading to differentiation/epigenetic modulation
additional_notes: Text notes weak potency and 'HDAC activity at high conc.'; range presented in µM though described as needing mM windows

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferases, promoting hypomethylation/epigenetic reprogramming
additional_notes: Existing use: AML/MDS; noted time-dependent effects and may need 96–120 h or repeated dosing for maximal effect

mechanism: Nucleoside analog that reduces DNA methylation (via DNMT inhibition after incorporation) affecting gene expression
additional_notes: Existing use: AML/MDS; noted schedule sensitivity and longer assay duration may show clearer effects

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and impairs autophagic flux
additional_notes: Rationale: autophagy dependence and combination rationale

mechanism: Targets ALDH-related vulnerabilities and increases oxidative stress; activity often enhanced with copper
additional_notes: Text notes often used with Cu2+; specifies ± copper: 0.1–1 µM CuCl2

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial respiratory chain complex I causing energetic stress and altered metabolism
additional_notes: Rationale: can impair AML metabolism in vitro; described as weak metabolic drug needing mM windows

mechanism: Reported to affect WNT/STAT3 signaling and act as a mitochondrial uncoupler in cancer contexts
additional_notes: Text notes WNT/STAT3/mitochondrial uncoupling; solubility/precipitation caution mentioned elsewhere in notes
